name: specify-a-feature
when_to_use: When the user needs a concrete, actionable plan for a feature.
When the flow should gather context, review architecture, and drive implementation details synchronously.

steps.description: Capture the feature goal and what success looks like.
steps.details: Interview the user about outcomes, corner cases, test cases, and any constraints that change the shape of the plan.

steps.description: Review the system context before deciding the feature shape.
steps.details: Use the system review flow to confirm the requirements, approach, and current constraints.

steps.description: Review the architecture context before choosing implementation details.
steps.details: Use the architecture review flow to confirm the entity model, relationships, invariants, and guidance that the feature must respect.

steps.description: Generate the implementation template and fill it out.
steps.details: Invoke the `specify-implementation` CLI endpoint, then complete the generated template with the feature decisions and reasoning.

steps.description: Decide on proposed PRs and fill out each PR template.
steps.details: Choose an appropriate set of proposed PRs, invoke the `specify-pr` flow for each one, and fill out those templates with the relevant intent, acceptance criteria, tests, and risks.

steps.description: Prompt the user to review the result.
steps.details: Show the draft plan back to the user and confirm that the scoped feature, implementation, and PR breakdown still match what they want.
